question: Q1: You need to add a dropdown field to a form schema. The options are "Yes", "No", and "N/A". Write the correct JSON for the options array. What happens if you use the wrong format?
answer: Correct format: options: [{label: "Yes", value: "yes"}, {label: "No", value: "no"}, {label: "N/A", value: "na"}]. Rule 9 states enum options must be {label, value} pairs. If you use string arrays like ["Yes", "No", "N/A"] instead, React will crash during rendering because the normalizeEnumOptions() function expects objects and fails on primitives.

question: Q2: A colleague suggests building a PDF parser to extract form data from USDA paper forms. Another suggests creating custom inputTypes like "currency" and "percentage". What do you tell them?
answer: Tell them both are dead approaches. PDF-parser is listed as having 60% accuracy (not viable). Custom inputTypes broke schemas in past attempts. Instead: supported-inputTypes-only are text|textarea|date|number|email|tel|acknowledgment|signature. For currency/percentage, use existing number type with guidance text in the schema. Forms-ARE-the-UI; schema-driven architecture drives both web and PDF from one source.

question: Q3: You added a new element type to UniversalFormRenderer on the web side. It works in the browser. Is the task done?
answer: No. Rule 10 requires UI/PDF-parity. You must also update pdf-template-generator.ts to ensure the element renders identically in PDF. Cross-cutting trace requires: UniversalFormRenderer.tsx (UI) + universal-html-template.ts (PDF) + pdf-template-generator.ts (types) match. Run /docs to regenerate PDF docs and verify both render identically before PR.

question: Q4: You need to build a new producer dashboard page. What is the very first thing you must do before writing any code?
answer: Rule 8 states UI-work-SEARCH-FIRST. Use /ui slash command or grep to find existing dashboard patterns in apps/web/src/components/dashboard/ and apps/web/src/components/layout/. Study PageContainer, DashboardHeader, StatsCard components. Then trace how pages are structured in app/producer/[page]/page.tsx before writing new code.

question: Q5: You write console.log("🚀 Successfully processed submission!") in a Fastify route. What is wrong?
answer: Rule 11 states no-slop (no-emoji-logs, no-AI-isms). Emoji in logs violates project standards. Remove the emoji and use plain text: console.log("Successfully processed submission"). Also ensure logging is appropriate for the context; over-logging in hot paths should be avoided.

question: Q6: Describe the monorepo structure. What are the apps and packages, what port does each service run on, and what is the package manager?
answer: Monorepo uses pnpm. Apps: apps/web (Next.js-16 on port 3000, React-19, Tailwind, shadcn/ui), apps/api (Fastify, TypeScript, Prisma-7 on port 4000, Swagger at localhost:4000/docs). Packages: packages/database (Prisma7+PostgreSQL, seeds, migrations), packages/sdk (auto-gen TS SDK from OpenAPI—do not edit src/), packages/sdk-python (Python SDK same spec), sprints/ (PM context, specs, feedback xlsx).

question: Q7: Explain what schema-driven architecture means in this project. Trace how a single JSON schema flows through the system.
answer: Schema-driven means: JSON form schema (59 files in packages/database/prisma/seeds/form-schemas/) -> UniversalFormRenderer (web UI) -> PDF generation. One schema drives both. Flow: FormTemplate.schema (JSON) -> json-schema-loader.ts loads it -> UniversalFormRenderer.tsx renders UI -> rules-based logic (stopForm, skipTo, subQuestions) -> getStyles() + renderFormField() -> pdf-template-generator.ts generates PDF from same schema -> Puppeteer renders -> PDF output. All forms-ARE-the-UI.

question: Q8: What are the two authentication methods supported by the API? When would you use each?
answer: JWT (Authorization: Bearer token) and API_Key (X-API-Key: certaxiom_live_...). Use JWT for user-facing web apps and SDK authentication (new Configuration({accessToken})). Use API_Key for service-to-service integration, webhooks, and backend automation where you need a long-lived credential without user login. Both routes respect role-based access control.

question: Q9: List the form series. What does each letter represent and how do they relate to OSP scopes?
answer: Four series: G (17 forms, Grower scope), H (14 forms, Handler scope), L (14 forms, Livestock scope), V (2 forms, Wine scope), Common (7 forms, cross-scope). G/H/L map to the single-OSP-multi-scope architecture where one OSP contains multiple scopes. V and Common are specialty/shared forms. Each series code links to CertificationScope enum (GROWER|HANDLER|LIVESTOCK|WINE|MUSHROOM|BROKER) in the FormTemplate.

question: Q10: What is the relationship between the PM repo and the source code repo? Why are they separate?
answer: PM repo (mmostagirbhuiyan/certaxiom-pm) contains GitHub Issues and sprint specs. Source code repo (mmostagirbhuiyan/certaxiom) contains all technical code. They are separate because non-tech stakeholders (product, business) have write access to PM repo but not source code repo. /pm slash command syncs sprint-spec -> GitHub-Issues for tech team visibility. Separation maintains governance: PM owns requirements, tech team owns implementation.

question: Q11: You need a list page with counts at the top and a detail/edit page. Which header component for each? What are the StatsCard requirements?
answer: List page: use DashboardHeader (WITH StatsCards). Detail/edit page: use PageHeader (WITHOUT StatsCards). StatsCard requirements: {title, value, subtitle} are REQUIRED (subtitle prevents uneven heights), plus optional icon and variant. Variant must be info|success|warning|organic—NOT default. StatsCard variant:default is forbidden on list pages because it breaks visual hierarchy.

question: Q12: How do you embed a data table inside a card component? What CSS classes are required?
answer: Structure: Card > CardContent.!p-0 > EnterpriseDataTable.border-0.bg-transparent.shadow-none.rounded-none. The !p-0 removes CardContent padding, and EnterpriseDataTable classes remove borders/bg/shadow/rounding so it visually merges with the card. This pattern appears in rule 66 and cross-cutting traces for entity listing pages.

question: Q13: You need to add a new API route for bulk-deleting materials. Describe the exact steps including what you must NOT skip.
answer: Steps: 1) Create src/routes/v1/materials-bulk-delete.ts with Fastify handler. 2) In src/routes/index.ts, register the route via app.register(). 3) Add OpenAPI schema metadata (@param, @response) to route handler. 4) DO NOT skip OpenAPI schema or SDK-regen will break. 5) Run pnpm --filter sdk generate -> pnpm --filter sdk build. 6) Update TASKS.md with files modified. 7) Test via app.inject({method:"DELETE",url:"/api/v1/materials-bulk"}) -> expect statusCode. 8) Commit with one PR per task.

question: Q14: Describe the seed data execution order and idempotency guarantee. How do you run seeds with expanded test data?
answer: Order: 00-cleanup(passkey-safe) -> 01-tenants -> 02-users -> 03-orgs -> 04-templates(59) -> 05-osp -> 07-workflows -> 08-13(phase61). All seeds use upsert, guaranteeing idempotency (safe to re-run). Run: pnpm --filter database seed (default). For expanded test data: pnpm --filter database seed --phase61 or --tags=perf:* (performance), cert:* (certification). Commands: pnpm --filter database seed --list shows all phases.

question: Q15: What is the testing philosophy? Write an example of a BAD test and a GOOD test for a Fastify route.
answer: Philosophy: rewrote-differently-same-behavior -> tests-pass? Test behavior, not implementation. BAD test: expect(source).toContain('fastify.post') or expect(mockPrisma.material.create).toHaveBeenCalled() (mocks implementation). GOOD test: const response = await app.inject({method:"POST", url:"/api/v1/materials", payload: {...}}); expect(response.statusCode).toBe(201); expect(JSON.parse(response.body)).toHaveProperty('id'). Verify actual HTTP behavior, not code structure.

question: Q16: Trace the complete submission workflow from creation to approval. Include the branch path for requested changes.
answer: Main path: DRAFT -> SUBMITTED -> ASSIGNED -> IN_REVIEW -> PARTIALLY_REVIEWED -> APPROVED. Branch path: IN_REVIEW -> REQUEST_CHANGES -> SUBMITTED (loops back). State machine implemented in services/workflow/submission-workflow.service.ts. Each status transition validates rules, updates timestamps (approvedAt?, expiredAt?), and triggers notifications. Submission tracks selectedScopes[], ospStatus, versionNumber. A submission can loop through REQUEST_CHANGES multiple times before APPROVED or REJECTED.

question: Q17: Trace the complete PDF generation flow from client request to rendered PDF. Name every file involved.
answer: Flow: routes/pdf-download.ts receives request -> calls pdf-generator.ts -> calls json-schema-loader.ts to load FormTemplate.schema -> calls universal-html-template.ts (in src/pdf-templates/) -> getStyles() generates CSS -> renderFormField() renders each element -> pdf-template-generator.ts generates final HTML -> Puppeteer renders HTML -> PDF output. Files: apps/api/src/routes/pdf-download.ts, apps/api/src/services/pdf-generator.ts, apps/api/src/services/json-schema-loader.ts, apps/api/src/pdf-templates/universal-html-template.ts, apps/api/src/services/pdf-template-generator.ts.

question: Q18: The API team added 3 new endpoints. What is the exact sequence to regenerate the SDK?
answer: Sequence: 1) Ensure API is running (pnpm dev includes api). 2) Routes must have OpenAPI schema metadata. 3) Run: pnpm --filter sdk generate (reads OpenAPI spec from localhost:4000/docs, regenerates src/ from OpenAPI). 4) Run: pnpm --filter sdk build (compiles TS). 5) For Python SDK: pnpm --filter sdk-python generate. DO NOT edit generated src/ files or change openapitools.json without re-testing regen.

question: Q19: You are running /sprint 3 but gh CLI is unavailable. What happens? What do you do when back on local?
answer: Offline handling: /sprint command has offline fallback. When gh CLI unavailable, sprint-ledger.json (at .claude/sprint-ledger.json) auto-captures logs and task tracking. Continue work; ledger stores all metadata offline. When gh CLI is back online, the next /sprint run auto-flushes ledger to GitHub Issues so no context is lost. Rule 14 guarantees sprint-ledger-offline-fallback(.claude/sprint-ledger.json).

question: Q20: Walk through sprint execution from planning to PR. What constraints must an agent follow?
answer: Planning: /pm syncs sprint specs from PM repo -> GitHub Issues. Execution: /sprint (Tech-Lead or Sonnet/Opus delegate by complexity) -> agent selects task -> researches & implements -> updates TASKS.md with files modified -> creates one PR per task. Constraints: one-PR/task, no-scope-creep, discovery-first (search patterns before coding), BLOCKED-escape (no dead-end attempts), no-emoji-logs, update-TASKS.md-with-files-modified, update-session-state-at-end. Each agent follows critical rules 1-14.

question: Q21: You need to add a new Equipment column to a form dataTable. Trace every file and package that must change.
answer: Files: 1) schema.prisma (add Equipment entity if not exists + migration). 2) packages/database/prisma/migrations/ (new migration). 3) packages/database/prisma/seeds/ (seed Equipment data). 4) apps/api/src/routes/v1/equipment.ts (CRUD routes + OpenAPI). 5) apps/api/src/routes/index.ts (register route). 6) Form schema JSON (add entityType:"Equipment" to dataTable element). 7) packages/sdk (regenerate SDK). 8) apps/web/src/hooks/useEntityCRUD (may add Equipment variant). 9) apps/web/src/components/forms/UniversalFormRenderer.tsx (render Equipment column). 10) TASKS.md (log changes).

question: Q22: A schema change adds a new field to the Organization model. List every downstream effect across the monorepo.
answer: Downstream: 1) schema.prisma (add field + migration). 2) packages/database/prisma/migrations/ (migration). 3) packages/database/prisma/seeds/03-organizations.ts (seed with new field). 4) apps/api/src/routes/org/org-profile.ts (add field to response DTO). 5) apps/api/src/routes/org/org-profile.ts (update PATCH handler to accept new field). 6) apps/api/src/routes/v1/organizations.ts (update GET/PATCH). 7) packages/sdk (regen SDK, @certaxiom/sdk types update). 8) apps/web/src/hooks/useOrganization (context may need update). 9) Profile form schema (if field is user-editable). 10) Form-field-mappings (if field maps to submission data). 11) TASKS.md (log all changes).

question: Q23: What are the three DataTable types and how does each store its data?
answer: Three types: 1) Entity (entityType:"Material", entityFilter:{...}) — fetches linked-entity-data-from-DB via entity loader, displays read-only rows. 2) FixedRows (fixedRows:[...]) — predefined rows in schema, saves to FormInstance.data as user edits. 3) JSON (fieldId:"...") — single fieldId value, saves to FormInstance.data[fieldId]. Rule 6 and section 70 define these patterns.

question: Q24: Explain the Single-OSP Multi-Scope architecture. How do the G, H, and L series map to it?
answer: Architecture: one OSP (Organic-System-Plan) contains multiple CertificationScopes. A producer selects which scopes apply (GROWER|HANDLER|LIVESTOCK). G series (17 forms, Grower scope) applies to producers growing crops. H series (14 forms, Handler scope) applies to producers processing/handling. L series (14 forms, Livestock scope) applies to producers raising livestock. Wizard is pre-cert only; post-cert producers access forms individually. selectedScopes[] on Submission tracks which scopes were included. This allows one OSP document to cover mixed operations (e.g., grower + handler).

question: Q25: How does the webhook system work across the API and SDK layers?
answer: Webhooks are documented in schema: Webhook entity in database (Prisma model), routes at PUBLIC_V1:/api/v1/webhooks (GET/POST/DELETE), SDK auto-generates @certaxiom/sdk WebhooksApi. Webhooks stored in DB with event type, target URL, credentials. API triggers webhooks on key events (submission state changes, form completion). SDK provides typed client: new WebhooksApi(config).createWebhook({event, url}). Webhooks enable external systems to listen for CertAxiom events without polling.

question: Q26: A subQuestion has a fieldId but no explicit type property. What happens if you only check element.type === "question"?
answer: It fails. Rule 10 states: check element.type==="question" OR (!element.type&&element.fieldId). SubQuestions inherit parent's behavior but lack explicit type. If you only check ===="question", subQuestions without type property will be skipped or mishandled in rendering/PDF. Must check both conditions: (element.type === "question" || (!element.type && element.fieldId)) to catch all fieldId-based elements including subQuestions.

question: Q27: You want to clear the database and re-seed. You run DELETE FROM on all tables. What breaks and what should you do instead?
answer: Breaks: foreign key constraints (Submission references FormInstance references FormTemplate references Organization references Tenant). Don't use DELETE FROM manually. Rule 124 states seeds use idempotent upsert. Correct approach: pnpm --filter database seed --phase00-cleanup runs 00-cleanup seed safely (passkey-safe cleanup), then subsequent phases rebuild all tables in dependency order. Or use prisma migrate reset (if safe in dev) to reset schema and re-seed atomically.

question: Q28: You add bg-background to a div inside AppShell. The page looks different. Why?
answer: Rule 67 states NO:bg-background-inside-AppShell(blocks-gradient). AppShell has a gradient background. Adding bg-background class blocks the gradient with a solid color, breaking the visual design. Remove bg-background from elements inside AppShell. Use bg-white, bg-transparent, or no background class instead.

question: Q29: You complete 3 sprint tasks but gh CLI is unavailable. You document in TASKS.md and move on. What is wrong?
answer: Incomplete logging. Rule 2 requires update-session-state-at-end. You must also update .claude/sprint-ledger.json (offline fallback) with task metadata (task IDs, PR links, files modified). Documenting only TASKS.md without session-state means the next sprint run cannot reconstruct your work context. Must log to both: TASKS.md (for human review) + sprint-ledger.json (for agent continuity).

question: Q30: You edit openapitools.json in the SDK package, commit, and two days later the SDK is broken. What did you forget?
answer: Forgot to regenerate and test. Rule from section 139 states DONT:change-openapitools.json-without-testing-regen. Editing openapitools.json changes OpenAPI code generation config. You must immediately run pnpm --filter sdk generate -> pnpm --filter sdk build and test that all types still compile. Two days later suggests the config change diverged from actual API, and later API changes broke the SDK because initial regen was skipped. Always regen immediately after config changes.
